what: Step 6: How to read annotation coverage
title: Case Study: Human RNA-seq Workflow
text: annotation_rate column proportion genes non-missing value field annotated gene table. main practical checks : symbol coverage high enough downstream symbol-based tools gene_name coverage usually track symbol coverage entrez_id can little lower, large gaps still deserve attention low rates suggest rerunning "hybrid" checking installed backends mock example, symbol gene_name fully covered, entrez_id coverage partial. pattern often acceptable next steps mainly depend symbols, still worth noting analysis record.

title: Case Study: Toy RNA-seq Workflow
text: case study shows expranno moves input tables saved analysis outputs. example uses bundled toy data, steps match real RNA-seq workflow.

what: Step 1: Start from expr and meta
title: Case Study: Toy RNA-seq Workflow
text: expression matrix one row per gene one column per sample gene_id. metadata table one row per sample starts sample.
code: demo$expr #>              gene_id sample_a sample_b sample_c #> 1 ENSG00000141510.17      120      140      118 #> 2 ENSG00000146648.18       80       77       91 #> 3 ENSG00000012048.23       25       30       21 demo$meta #>     sample   group batch #> 1 sample_a    case    b1 #> 2 sample_b control    b1 #> 3 sample_c    case    b2

text: website build, use annotation_engine = "none" article can render without external annotation services. real analysis, recommended setting "hybrid". practice, production call :
code: anno <- annotate_expr(   expr = demo$expr,   meta = demo$meta,   species = "human",   annotation_engine = "none" )  anno$expr_anno #>          gene_id_raw         gene_id symbol gene_name entrez_id biotype #> 1 ENSG00000141510.17 ENSG00000141510     NA        NA        NA      NA #> 2 ENSG00000146648.18 ENSG00000146648     NA        NA        NA      NA #> 3 ENSG00000012048.23 ENSG00000012048     NA        NA        NA      NA #>   chromosome start end strand annotation_source annotation_status sample_a #> 1         NA    NA  NA     NA              <NA>       unannotated      120 #> 2         NA    NA  NA     NA              <NA>       unannotated       80 #> 3         NA    NA  NA     NA              <NA>       unannotated       25 #>   sample_b sample_c #> 1      140      118 #> 2       77       91 #> 3       30       21 anno <- annotate_expr(   expr = expr,   meta = meta,   species = "human",   annotation_engine = "hybrid",   output_file = "expr_anno.csv" )

title: Getting Started with expranno
text: workflow easiest read four checkpoints: validate input contract annotate genes inspect coverage merge expression metadata run Deconvolution Signature analyses merged table

title: Getting Started with expranno
text: Use species = "human" ENSG... input IDs species = "mouse" ENSMUSG... input IDs. IDs clean Ensembl IDs, species = "auto" can infer .
code: human_demo <- example_expranno_data("human") mouse_demo <- example_expranno_data("mouse")  head(human_demo$expr$gene_id) #> [1] "ENSG00000141510.17" "ENSG00000146648.18" "ENSG00000012048.23" head(mouse_demo$expr$gene_id) #> [1] "ENSMUSG00000059552.8"  "ENSMUSG00000020122.15" "ENSMUSG00000017167.16"

title: Getting Started with expranno
text: expranno exposes five annotation modes: "hybrid": recommended production mode "biomart": use biomaRt "orgdb": use org.Hs.eg.db org.Mm.eg.db "ensdb": use EnsDb "none": skip annotation lookup keep normalized IDs "hybrid" default coverage-oriented mode. uses cascade: biomaRt species-specific orgdb species-specific EnsDb lightweight docs tests, examples use "none". --one wrapper run_expranno().
code: annotated <- annotate_expr(   expr = demo$expr,   meta = demo$meta,   species = "human",   annotation_engine = "none" )  merged <- merge_expr_meta(   expr_anno = annotated$expr_anno,   meta = annotated$meta_checked ) result <- run_expranno(   expr = demo$expr,   meta = demo$meta,   species = "human",   annotation_engine = "none",   output_dir = tempdir(),   run_deconvolution = FALSE,   run_signature = FALSE )  result #> <expranno_result> #>   annotated genes: 3 #>   merged rows: 9 #>   deconvolution runs: 0 #>   signature runs: 0

what: What to inspect after a run
title: Getting Started with expranno
text: production analysis "hybrid", first things check : expr_anno.csv annotation coverage report annotate_expr() expr_meta_merged.csv symbol coverage weak, downstream Deconvolution Signature methods usually first places problem appears.

title: Theory and Design
text: package aims four things well: enforce simple strict input contract maximize annotation coverage human mouse keep expression metadata aligned make downstream immune signature analyses reproducible

what: Why the package starts with annotation
title: Theory and Design
text: Many downstream tools expect gene symbols rather Ensembl IDs. reliable annotation layer makes possible : keep original gene_id recover display-ready symbol attach gene-level metadata name biotype create symbol-based matrices deconvolution scoring

title: Theory and Design
text: default design coverage-first cascade: biomaRt org.Hs.eg.db org.Mm.eg.db optional EnsDb packages stage fills missing fields left previous stage. expranno can often recover usable annotation single-source lookup.

title: Theory and Design
text: expranno exposes five annotation engines.

title: Theory and Design
text: recommended default. designed maximize annotation coverage filling missing fields progressively across multiple sources. Human: biomaRt org.Hs.eg.db optional EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86 Mouse: biomaRt org.Mm.eg.db optional EnsDb.Mmusculus.v79 Use goal highest practical annotation rate.

title: Theory and Design
text: uses biomaRt. convenient want one consistent Ensembl-oriented source, complete mode fields missing.

title: Theory and Design
text: uses org.Hs.eg.db org.Mm.eg.db. useful stable symbol identifier mapping, aim richest single-source annotation path.

title: Theory and Design
text: uses EnsDb. especially useful coordinate biotype-oriented annotation fields.

title: Theory and Design
text: skips external lookup keeps normalized Ensembl IDs. mainly useful tests, examples, lightweight documentation builds.

title: Theory and Design
text: Use "hybrid" real analysis. Use "biomart" want single online Ensembl source. Use "orgdb" need local annotation mapping layer. Use "ensdb" gene model fields main target. Use "none" dry runs, docs, testing package flow without external dependencies.

what: Why annotation coverage is a first-class output
title: Theory and Design
text: expranno treat annotation hidden preprocessing step. coverage report part workflow answers important downstream question immediately: annotation complete enough symbol-based analyses? practice, coverage report checked running Deconvolution Signature analysis. Strong symbol gene_name coverage usually indicates expression matrix ready steps, weak coverage often sign species backend configuration needs attention.

title: Theory and Design
text: expranno keeps two complementary representations: wide annotated matrix file export long merged table downstream analysis wide representation best exchange reproducibility: expr_anno.csv long representation best plotting, grouped summaries, sample-level joins: expr_meta_merged.csv

title: Theory and Design
text: annotation merging, package supports: immune deconvolution immunedeconv pathway signature scoring GSVA ssGSEA designed write explicit CSV outputs results easy inspect outside R.

title: Theory and Design
text: production analysis, usual order : validate expr meta inspect expr_anno.csv inspect annotation coverage report inspect expr_meta_merged.csv run Deconvolution run Signature analysis

title: Authors and Citation
text: Daiki User. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: User D (2026). expranno: Expression Annotation, Metadata Integration, Deconvolution, Signature Analysis. R package version 2.00.
code: @Manual{,   title = {expranno: Expression Annotation, Metadata Integration, Deconvolution, and Signature Analysis},   author = {Daiki User},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 2.00}, }

title: expranno
text: expranno R package RNA-seq workflows start expression matrix sample metadata, move gene annotation, metadata integration, Deconvolution, Signature analysis. package designed human mouse expression matrices : first column expr gene_id remaining columns expr samples first column meta sample expranno built around four jobs: gene annotation Ensembl IDs expression metadata integration Deconvolution immunedeconv Signature analysis GSVA ssGSEA Rather forcing single annotation backend, expranno uses coverage-first strategy can combine: biomaRt org.Hs.eg.db org.Mm.eg.db optional EnsDb packages makes possible recover symbols, names, identifiers single-source annotation pass. expranno simple analysis sequence: validate expr meta annotate genes write expr_anno.csv merge expression metadata expr_meta_merged.csv run Deconvolution save one table per method run Signature analysis save one score table per method
code: flowchart TB   subgraph inputs["Inputs"]     expr["exprgene x sample table"]     meta["metasample x metadata table"]   end    subgraph core["Core workflow"]     validate["Validatecheck required columns"]     annotate["Annotationnormalize Ensembl IDshybrid human or mouse mapping"]     report["Coverage reportannotation_rate by field"]     merge["Mergecombine expr_anno and meta"]   end    subgraph outputs["Saved outputs"]     expranno["expr_anno.csv"]     merged["expr_meta_merged.csv"]   end    subgraph downstream["Downstream analyses"]     deconv["Deconvolutionimmunedeconvone CSV per method"]     signature["SignatureGSVA or ssGSEAone CSV per method"]   end    expr --> validate   meta --> validate   validate --> annotate   annotate --> expranno   annotate --> report   expranno --> merge   meta --> merge   merge --> merged   merged --> deconv   merged --> signature    classDef input fill:#eef5fb,stroke:#315c86,color:#17324d,stroke-width:1.5px;   classDef process fill:#ffffff,stroke:#315c86,color:#17324d,stroke-width:1.5px;   classDef output fill:#eaf6ef,stroke:#3c7a57,color:#17324d,stroke-width:1.5px;    class expr,meta input;   class validate,annotate,report,merge,deconv,signature process;   class expranno,merged output;

title: expranno
text: Local install: Optional backends:
code: # install.packages("pak") # pak::pak("path/to/expranno") BiocManager::install(c(   "biomaRt",   "AnnotationDbi",   "org.Hs.eg.db",   "org.Mm.eg.db",   "ensembldb",   "EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86",   "EnsDb.Mmusculus.v79",   "immunedeconv",   "GSVA" ))

title: expranno
text: main wrapper run_expranno(). orchestrates whole pipeline raw inputs saved outputs.
code: result <- expranno::run_expranno(   expr = expr,   meta = meta,   species = "human",   annotation_engine = "hybrid",   output_dir = "results",   run_deconvolution = TRUE,   run_signature = TRUE,   geneset_file = "hallmark.gmt",   signature_method = "both" )

title: expranno
text: expr must gene--sample table. meta must sample metadata table.
code: demo <- expranno::example_expranno_data() head(demo$expr) head(demo$meta)

title: expranno
text: expranno intentionally split one end--end wrapper four stepwise building blocks. run_expranno() annotate_expr() merge_expr_meta() run_cell_deconvolution() run_signature_analysis()

title: expranno
code: demo <- expranno::example_expranno_data()  res <- expranno::run_expranno(   expr = demo$expr,   meta = demo$meta,   species = "human",   annotation_engine = "none",   output_dir = tempdir(),   run_deconvolution = FALSE,   run_signature = FALSE )

title: expranno
text: expranno designed write analysis-ready CSV files stable names. expr_anno.csv expr_meta_merged.csv cell_deconv_<method>.csv signature_gsva.csv signature_ssgsea.csv high-level wrapper run_expranno(), package also exposes smaller steps: annotate_expr() merge_expr_meta() run_cell_deconvolution() run_signature_analysis()

title: expranno
text: real run annotation_engine = "hybrid", first files tables inspect : expr_anno.csv annotation coverage report returned annotate_expr() expr_meta_merged.csv practical checklist : confirm symbol coverage high enough downstream symbol-based tools confirm gene_name coverage tracks symbol confirm merged sample labels line expected meta rows use merged table common input Deconvolution Signature analyses

title: expranno
text: package website includes: getting started article step--step case study tutorial theory design article function reference

title: Annotate an expression matrix — annotate_expr
text: Validates expr meta, normalizes Ensembl IDs, infers accepts species, runs coverage-first annotation workflow, binds annotation columns expression table, optionally writes expr_anno.csv.

title: Annotate an expression matrix — annotate_expr
code: annotate_expr(   expr,   meta,   species = c("auto", "human", "mouse"),   annotation_engine = c("hybrid", "biomart", "orgdb", "ensdb", "none"),   fields = NULL,   strip_version = TRUE,   output_file = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Annotate an expression matrix — annotate_expr
